title: 30-day privilege-escalation audit
difficulty_justification: Two-step plan: rbac.list_permissions to confirm authority, then docs.retrieve plus telemetry.query_timeseries (or equivalent audit pull) to enumerate elevation events over the 30-day window.
query_text: Generate a 30-day audit report of every privilege-escalation event - any user who gained access beyond their base role, whether granted, denied, or revoked. I want a count by role pair and the raw events.

eval_criteria.gold_answer: 14 escalation events in the last 30 days. By transition: scientific_user -> tier2_privileged temporary grants 9 (8 approved, 1 denied); researcher -> tier3_restricted 3 (2 approved, 1 revoked at day 14); sysadmin -> tier4_sensitive 2 (both granted by facility director, 30-day cap). All approved grants are time-limited and within policy SLA (median 1.4 business days). One anomaly: 1 revocation triggered by suspicious cross-project access pattern (user rliu, day 14).

checkpoints.description: Agent confirms its role permits a privilege-escalation audit.
Agent pulls events spanning a 30-day window.
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: trace contains a tool call whose time range covers >=30 days

checkpoints.description: Final answer reports counts grouped by (from_role -> to_tier).
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: final_answer contains at least three role-pair groupings with numeric counts summing to ~14
